title: WebEx: Taking Conferencing to the Business Communications Summit
author: Peter S. Kastner
source_file: 2002 WebEx_ Taking Conferencing to the Business Communications Summit PSK.pdf
abstract: This Profile examines WebEx Communications, Inc.'s conferencing platform and its private-network infrastructure approach to delivering data-rich online business meetings. The study covers WebEx Meeting Center, WebEx OnCall, WebEx OnStage, and the forthcoming WebEx Training Center, arguing that WebEx's utility-grade infrastructure positions it as the leader in business communications.
importance_rationale: Published at the peak of WebEx's growth phase in 2002, this Aberdeen Profile by Peter S. Kastner identified WebEx as the clear market leader in online conferencing; WebEx was subsequently acquired by Cisco for $3.2B in 2007, validating this assessment's significance.
relevance_rationale: WebEx's product architecture (Meeting Center, OnCall, OnStage, Training Center) prefigures every major collaboration platform today; the private-network vs. public-internet infrastructure debate maps directly to modern UCaaS quality-of-service questions and is directly applicable to Zoom vs. Teams vs. Google Meet analysis.
prescience_rationale: Kastner's identification of WebEx as the conferencing market leader proved exactly correct — WebEx commanded the enterprise market until Cisco's $3.2B acquisition in 2007, and the platform survives today as Cisco Webex, one of the top three UCaaS platforms globally.
